steps.text: Determine the response time (RT) of the system.
Inject zero air into the system.
Measure the native stack concentration of the species to be spiked.
Determine a dilution factor (DF) for each dynamic spike.
Obtain reference spectra for each analyte, interferant, surrogate, CTS, and tracer.

Report traceability and other pertinent information for each reference spectrum, for each compound, including temperature, pressure, concentration, cylinder source and specifications, spectral regions of analysis used for quantitation, and calibration fit equations and correlations.
Report the FTIR spectral resolution, line position, cell pathlength, temperature, pressure, and apodization function.
Perform testing at a single point that most closely matches the mean.
Sample using the stratification check procedure specified in section 11.5.1.1 of this method.
Assign a unique filename to each spectrum and separately to each corresponding interferogram.
Measure the temperature of the gas cell directly.
Measure the gas cell pressure empirically.
Record cell pathlength, pressure, temperature, laser frequency, and cylinder regulator type.
Record software/algorithm related items: gases included in the analysis, concentration values of reference spectra, and analysis wavelength regions.
Record CTS, QA/QC and validation related items: list of spiked compounds, dynamic spiking status, spike introduction location, and standards quality.

preconditions.text: The concentration has stabilized when variability appears constant for five minutes.
Commercially prepared, or other available reference libraries are used to quantify data.
Traceable reference spectra provided by the FTIR manufacturer do not bracket the concentration of a particular compound.
The concentration at each traverse point differs from the mean concentration for all traverse points by no more than 5.0% of the mean concentration.
The criteria for single point sampling is not met, but the concentration at each traverse point differs from the mean concentration by no more than 10% of the mean.
The concentration at any traverse point differs from the mean by more than 10%.

constraints.text: Your spike gas flow rate must not contribute more than 10% of the total volumetric flow rate through the FTIR.
If spiking is not performed after each run and the post-test spike fails, all data for that test are invalid.
Resolution, line position, and apodization function used for collection of sample spectra must be the same as those of the reference spectra used for quantitation.
Reference spectra for each target compound must bracket the concentration of that compound in the sample stream.
Spectra and interferograms must be providable in '.spc' format upon request.
The temperature measurement device must be calibrated to within ±0.1 °C every 12 months.
The measurement device must be calibrated to within ±1 mmHg every 12 months.
Qualified surrogates should be appropriate for the compound actually being measured.
It is preferable that the compound of interest always be spiked if it is available as a certified standard.
Standards used for QA spiking must be of appropriate quality (e.g., ±2% for Protocol gases where available and ±5% for other certified gases).
